%%render long 2
B=3 #m
L_p=4 #m
theta=40 #°
L=6 #m
y_c= L+L_p/2 #m
h_c = y_c*sin(radians(theta)) #c
A = B*L_p #m2
F_= rho_water*g*h_c*A #N
I_c = (B*L_p**3)/12 #m4
y_p = (A * y_c**2 + I_c) / (A * y_c) #m

text/latex.content:
\[
\begin{aligned}
B &= 3 \; \;\textrm{(m)}
\\[10pt]
L_{p} &= 4 \; \;\textrm{(m)}
\\[10pt]
\theta &= 40 \; \;\textrm{(°)}
\\[10pt]
L &= 6 \; \;\textrm{(m)}
\\[10pt]
y_{c} &= L + \frac{ L_{p} }{ 2 } \\&= 6 + \frac{ 4 }{ 2 } \\&= 8.00 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
h_{c} &= y_{c} \cdot \sin { \left( \operatorname{radians} \theta \right) } \\&= 8.00 \cdot \sin { \left( \operatorname{radians} 40 \right) } \\&= 5.14 \; \;\textrm{(c)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
A &= B \cdot L_{p} \\&= 3 \cdot 4 \\&= 12 \; \;\textrm{(m2)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
F_{} &= \rho_{water} \cdot g \cdot h_{c} \cdot A \\&= 1000 \cdot 9.81 \cdot 5.14 \cdot 12 \\&= 605351.66 \; \;\textrm{(N)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
I_{c} &= \frac{ B \cdot \left( L_{p} \right) ^{ 3 } }{ 12 } \\&= \frac{ 3 \cdot \left( 4 \right) ^{ 3 } }{ 12 } \\&= 16.00 \; \;\textrm{(m4)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
y_{p} &= \frac{ A \cdot \left( y_{c} \right) ^{ 2 } + I_{c} }{ A \cdot y_{c} } \\&= \frac{ 12 \cdot \left( 8.00 \right) ^{ 2 } + 16.00 }{ 12 \cdot 8.00 } \\&= 8.17 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\end{aligned}
\]

%%render long 2
h_1=4 #m
d=2 #m
theta=55 #°
y_1 = h_1/sin(radians(theta)) #m
y_c = y_1+d/2 #m
h_c = y_c*sin(radians(theta)) #m
A= pi*(d/2)**2 #m2
F= rho_water*g*h_c*A/1000 #kN

I_c= (pi*(d/2)**4)/4 #m4
y_p= (A*y_c**2+I_c)/(y_c*A) #m

text/latex.content:
\[
\begin{aligned}
h_{1} &= 4 \; \;\textrm{(m)}
\\[10pt]
d &= 2 \; \;\textrm{(m)}
\\[10pt]
\theta &= 55 \; \;\textrm{(°)}
\\[10pt]
y_{1} &= \frac{ h_{1} }{ \sin { \left( \operatorname{radians} \theta \right) } } \\&= \frac{ 4 }{ \sin { \left( \operatorname{radians} 55 \right) } } \\&= 4.88 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
y_{c} &= y_{1} + \frac{ d }{ 2 } \\&= 4.88 + \frac{ 2 }{ 2 } \\&= 5.88 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
h_{c} &= y_{c} \cdot \sin { \left( \operatorname{radians} \theta \right) } \\&= 5.88 \cdot \sin { \left( \operatorname{radians} 55 \right) } \\&= 4.82 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
A &= \pi \cdot \left( \frac{ d }{ 2 } \right) ^{ 2 } \\&= 3.14 \cdot \left( \frac{ 2 }{ 2 } \right) ^{ 2 } \\&= 3.14 \; \;\textrm{(m2)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
F &= \rho_{water} \cdot g \cdot h_{c} \cdot \frac{ A }{ 1000 } \\&= 1000 \cdot 9.81 \cdot 4.82 \cdot \frac{ 3.14 }{ 1000 } \\&= 148.52 \; \;\textrm{(kN)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
I_{c} &= \frac{ \pi \cdot \left( \frac{ d }{ 2 } \right) ^{ 4 } }{ 4 } \\&= \frac{ 3.14 \cdot \left( \frac{ 2 }{ 2 } \right) ^{ 4 } }{ 4 } \\&= 0.79 \; \;\textrm{(m4)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
y_{p} &= \frac{ A \cdot \left( y_{c} \right) ^{ 2 } + I_{c} }{ y_{c} \cdot A } \\&= \frac{ 3.14 \cdot \left( 5.88 \right) ^{ 2 } + 0.79 }{ 5.88 \cdot 3.14 } \\&= 5.93 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\end{aligned}
\]

%%render long 2
AB= 4#m
BC= 2#m 
theta = 135 #°
theta_1 = 180 - theta #°
y_c = AB+BC/2 #m
A = pi*(BC**2)/4 #m2
h_c = y_c*sin(radians(theta_1)) #m
F = rho_water*g*h_c*A #N
r = BC/2 #m
I_c = pi*r**4/4 #m4
y_p= (A*y_c**2+I_c)/(y_c*A) #m

text/latex.content:
\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{AB} &= 4 \; \;\textrm{(m)}
\\[10pt]
\mathrm{BC} &= 2 \; \;\textrm{(m)}
\\[10pt]
\theta &= 135 \; \;\textrm{(°)}
\\[10pt]
\theta_{1} &= 180 - \theta \\&= 180 - 135 \\&= 45 \; \;\textrm{(°)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
y_{c} &= \mathrm{AB} + \frac{ \mathrm{BC} }{ 2 } \\&= 4 + \frac{ 2 }{ 2 } \\&= 5.00 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
A &= \pi \cdot \frac{ \left( \mathrm{BC} \right) ^{ 2 } }{ 4 } \\&= 3.14 \cdot \frac{ \left( 2 \right) ^{ 2 } }{ 4 } \\&= 3.14 \; \;\textrm{(m2)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
h_{c} &= y_{c} \cdot \sin { \left( \operatorname{radians} \theta_{1} \right) } \\&= 5.00 \cdot \sin { \left( \operatorname{radians} 45 \right) } \\&= 3.54 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
F &= \rho_{water} \cdot g \cdot h_{c} \cdot A \\&= 1000 \cdot 9.81 \cdot 3.54 \cdot 3.14 \\&= 108961.70 \; \;\textrm{(N)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
r &= \frac{ \mathrm{BC} }{ 2 } \\&= \frac{ 2 }{ 2 } \\&= 1.00 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
I_{c} &= \pi \cdot \frac{ \left( r \right) ^{ 4 } }{ 4 } \\&= 3.14 \cdot \frac{ \left( 1.00 \right) ^{ 4 } }{ 4 } \\&= 0.79 \; \;\textrm{(m4)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
y_{p} &= \frac{ A \cdot \left( y_{c} \right) ^{ 2 } + I_{c} }{ y_{c} \cdot A } \\&= \frac{ 3.14 \cdot \left( 5.00 \right) ^{ 2 } + 0.79 }{ 5.00 \cdot 3.14 } \\&= 5.05 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\end{aligned}
\]

\[
\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{AB} &= 3 \; \;\textrm{(m)}
\\[10pt]
\mathrm{BC} &= 1 \; \;\textrm{(m)}
\\[10pt]
\theta &= 120 \; \;\textrm{(°)}
\\[10pt]
\theta_{1} &= 180 - \theta \\&= 180 - 120 \\&= 60 \; \;\textrm{(°)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
y_{c} &= \mathrm{AB} + \mathrm{BC} - 4 \cdot \frac{ \mathrm{BC} }{ 3 \cdot \pi } \\&= 3 + 1 - 4 \cdot \frac{ 1 }{ 3 \cdot 3.14 } \\&= 3.58 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
A &= \pi \cdot \frac{ \left( \mathrm{BC} \right) ^{ 2 } }{ 2 } \\&= 3.14 \cdot \frac{ \left( 1 \right) ^{ 2 } }{ 2 } \\&= 1.57 \; \;\textrm{(m2)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
h_{c} &= y_{c} \cdot \sin { \left( \operatorname{radians} \theta_{1} \right) } \\&= 3.58 \cdot \sin { \left( \operatorname{radians} 60 \right) } \\&= 3.10 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
F &= \rho_{water} \cdot g \cdot h_{c} \cdot A \\&= 1000 \cdot 9.81 \cdot 3.10 \cdot 1.57 \\&= 47716.31 \; \;\textrm{(N)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
I &= 0.1098 \cdot \left( \mathrm{BC} \right) ^{ 4 } \\&= 0.1098 \cdot \left( 1 \right) ^{ 4 } \\&= 0.11 \; \;\textrm{(m4)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
y_{p} &= \frac{ A \cdot \left( y_{c} \right) ^{ 2 } + I }{ y_{c} \cdot A } \\&= \frac{ 1.57 \cdot \left( 3.58 \right) ^{ 2 } + 0.11 }{ 3.58 \cdot 1.57 } \\&= 3.60 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\end{aligned}

children.name: Dropdown
children.value:
In this problem, both the air (cylinder A) and the water (cylinder B) will be compressed and the weigth of the cylinder must be balanced by the force exerted by the fluid.  

In the case of cylinder A, the gas compression can be calculated with the perfect gas law , where temperature is the same and the volume reduces.  

In the case of cylinder B, the compressibiity of water can be calculated with the bulk modulus equation.

children.name: Dropdown
children.value:
**Cylinder A:**

$$
\frac{p}{\rho}=R_s T
$$

Assuming no change in T:

$$
\frac{p_1}{\rho_1}=\frac{p_2}{\rho_2}\\
\rho_2=p_2 \frac{\rho_1}{p_1}
$$  

From the ICAO standard atmosphere table, $\rho_1=1.225 kg/m^3$ and $p_1=101325 Pa$

From conservation of mass:

$$
\rho_1 Vol_1 =\rho_2 Vol_2\\
\rho_1 \cdot A \cdot L_1 =\rho_2 \cdot A \cdot L_2
$$

Solving for $L_2$:  

$$
L_2=  \frac{\rho_1 L_1}{\rho_2}
$$  
  
     
    

**Cylinder B:**

Considering:

$$
-\Delta p= E_v \frac{\Delta v}{v}\\
v= 1/\rho_{water}
$$

and, From the table of the physical properties of common liquids at standard sea-level atmospheric conditions at 15°C, $E_v= 2.14*10^9 Pa$  

$\Delta v$ can be solved.  
Then, considering that:

$$ 
v_2=v+\Delta v\\
\rho_2=\frac{1}{v_2}
$$  

and from conservation of mass:

$$
\rho_1 \cdot Vol_1 =\rho_2 \cdot Vol_2\\
\rho_1 \cdot A \cdot L_1 =\rho_2 \cdot AL2
$$

$L_2$ can be calculated as:

X_circle = 4*r/(3*pi) #m

text/latex.content:
\[
\begin{aligned}
X_{circle} &= 4 \cdot \frac{ r }{ 3 \cdot \pi } \\&= 4 \cdot \frac{ 1 }{ 3 \cdot 3.142 } \\&= 0.424 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
X_{rect} &= \frac{ r }{ 2 } \\&= \frac{ 1 }{ 2 } \\&= 0.500 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]

\\[10pt]
W_{rect} &= \rho_{water} \cdot g \cdot B \cdot r \cdot h_{water} \\&= 1000 \cdot 9.810 \cdot 3 \cdot 1 \cdot 4 \\&= 117720.000 \; \;\textrm{(N)}\\[10pt]
\\[10pt]
X &= \frac{ W_{circle} \cdot X_{circle} + W_{rect} \cdot X_{rect} }{ W_{circle} + W_{rect} } \\&= \frac{ 23114.268 \cdot 0.424 + 117720.000 \cdot 0.500 }{ 23114.268 + 117720.000 } \\&= 0.488 \; \;\textrm{(m)}\\[10pt]
\end{aligned}
\]
